content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks, which rely on iterative single-perspective self-feedback to improve outputs without extra training, can be enhanced by integrating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives at the core of modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms to reduce confirmation bias in self-feedback loops. This modification would allow Self-Refine to catch critical flaws in early outputs that a single self-aligned perspective would miss, while preserving its core advantage of requiring no supervised training or fine-tuning.
